record.field: Woodpecker CI is a simple, lightweight CI/CD engine that runs pipelines inside containers, wired to your Gitea, Forgejo, GitHub or GitLab repositories.

Woodpecker CI is a simple, lightweight CI/CD engine that runs pipelines inside containers, wired to your Gitea, Forgejo, GitHub or GitLab repositories.

Woodpecker is a community fork of Drone that keeps continuous integration deliberately small and container-first. You connect it to a Git forge over OAuth, commit a plain YAML pipeline (.woodpecker.yml) next to your code, and every push or pull request runs its steps as isolated containers. Its server/agent design lets a tiny server coordinate work while one or more agents execute the actual builds, so it scales by simply adding agents and stays light enough to self-host on modest hardware.

Key features
  • Container-native pipelines defined as YAML committed alongside your source code
  • Native OAuth integration with Gitea, Forgejo, GitHub, GitLab and Bitbucket forges
  • Server/agent architecture: lightweight agents pick up workflows and run each step in its own container
  • Matrix builds, parallel and conditional steps, and per-repository encrypted secrets
  • Reusable plugins packaged as containers for publishing, notifications and deployments
  • Small, resource-light footprint that scales horizontally by adding more agents
Best for

Small teams and self-hosters who want a lightweight, container-first CI/CD engine sitting next to a self-hosted Git forge. Woodpecker must be paired with a forge: register an OAuth application there, set WOODPECKER_HOST to the instance's public URL and fill the forge client/secret before the first login. Note that the agent mounts the host Docker socket, which grants it high privilege, so isolate the runner on a dedicated host for sensitive workloads.

services: woodpecker-server: image: woodpeckerci/woodpecker-server:latest environment: WOODPECKER_OPEN: "true" WOODPECKER_HOST: {{ woodpecker_host }} WOODPECKER_AGENT_SECRET: {{ woodpecker_agent_secret }} WOODPECKER_GITEA: "true" WOODPECKER_GITEA_URL: {{ woodpecker_gitea_url }} WOODPECKER_GITEA_CLIENT: {{ woodpecker_gitea_client }} WOODPECKER_GITEA_SECRET: {{ woodpecker_gitea_secret }} volumes: - ./woodpecker_server_data:/var/lib/woodpecker ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8000' container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network woodpecker-agent: image: woodpeckerci/woodpecker-agent:latest command: agent restart: unless-stopped depends_on: - woodpecker-server environment: WOODPECKER_SERVER: woodpecker-server:9000 WOODPECKER_AGENT_SECRET: {{ woodpecker_agent_secret }} WOODPECKER_MAX_WORKFLOWS: "2" volumes: - ./woodpecker_agent_config:/etc/woodpecker - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_agent' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
Public base URL of this Woodpecker instance, used for OAuth callbacks and pipeline links.
Shared secret that authenticates agents to the Woodpecker server.
Base URL of the Gitea/Forgejo forge Woodpecker connects to.
OAuth application client ID issued by the forge for Woodpecker.
OAuth application client secret issued by the forge for Woodpecker.
